Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 012 [Bit Rimki Varia][via cams/gkab]

Obverse

o io i  (13 lines missing)
o i 1'1'

[...] LI

(o i 1') [...] ... [...] his father [...] powerful [...] ... walks and [...] ... [...] ... their ... [...] to enemy country [...] they ....

[...] AD-šu₂

o i 3'3'

[...] ⸢A₂?⸣.GAL₂

o i 4'4'

o i 5'5'

[...] ⸢IG?⸣ KI

o i 6'6'

[...] U₂

o i 7'7'

[...]-šu₂-nu

o i 8'8'

[...] x DA?

o i 9'9'

[...] ana KUR KI.BAL :

o i 10'10'

[...] x-MEŠ-ni

o i 11'11'

[...] x tu-šak-la

(o i 11') You hold back [...] ....

o i 12'12'

[...] MU

(o i 12') [...] ... [...] ....

o i 13'13'

[...] x NIG₂ IGI

o i 14'14'

[...] dUTU

(o i 14') [...] Šamaš [...] speaks thus ...:

o i 15'15'

[...] x kam DU₁₁.GA

o i 16'16'

[...] TU U₂ A

(o i 16') '[...] ....'

o i 17'17'

[...] kam DU₁₁.GA

(o i 17') [...] speaks thus [...]:

o i 18'18'

[...] UN-MEŠ ma-da-a-tu₂

(o i 18') '[...] of numerous people.'

o i 19'19'

[...] kam DU₁₁.GA

(o i 19') [...] speaks thus [...]:

o i 20'20'

[...] x mi-na-tu-šu₂

(o i 20') '[...] ... his limbs [...] of the lord.'

o i 21'21'

[...] be-li₂

o i 22'22'

[...] x kam DU₁₁.GA

(o i 22') [...] speaks thus ...:

o i 23'23'

[...] TU? a-a im-ḫur

(o i 23') 'May he not receive [...] ....

o i 24'24'

[...] x SAG

(o i 24') '[...] ... [...] ... [...].'

o i 25'25'

[...] x

o i 26'26'

[...]

o i 27'27'

[... DU₁₁].⸢GA⸣

(o i 27') [...] speaks [...]:

o i 28'28'

[...] x LU

(o i 28') '[...] ....'

o i 29'29'

[...] DU₁₁.GA

(o i 29') [...] speaks [...]:

o i 30'30'

[...]

(o i 30') '[...]. May the curses in the mouths of numerous people be moved away.'

Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

[x x lu]-u₂ dup-pu-ra iz-zi-ir-<et> KA UN-MEŠ ma-⸢aʾ⸣-[da-a]-tu₂

o ii 22

[...] ana SU kam ⸢DU₁₁⸣.[GA]

(o ii 2) [...] speaks thus to the body:

o ii 33

[x SU]-⸢ia₅⸣ lu-u₂ dup-pu-ra ṣu-lum-mat IGI-MEŠ-⸢ia₅?⸣

(o ii 3) 'May the anger at me [...] my body be moved away.'

o ii 44

[...] ŠA₃.MAḪ kam DU₁₁.GA

(o ii 4) [...] speaks thus [...] the large intestine:

o ii 55

[x x] ⸢na⸣-as-ḫa-ma lu-u₂ tab-ka-a-ma

(o ii 5) '[...] the complaints in the mouths of numerous people be torn out and may they be poured out.'

o ii 66

[ta]-⸢zi⸣-im-tu₄ KA UN-MEŠ ma-aʾ-da-a-ti

o ii 77

[...] ana qer-bi kam DU₁₁.GA

(o ii 7) [...] speaks thus to the innards:

o ii 88

[lu]-u₂ qur-ru-bu-nim-ma ik-rib šul-mi u TI.LA

(o ii 8) 'May prayers of well-being and life be brought near to me.

o ii 99

⸢IGI⸣ dUTU ana-ku lul-qe₂

(o ii 9) 'May I receive (them) before Šamaš.'

o ii 1010

⸢NUN⸣ ana UGU 14 <NIG₂>.GAL₂.LA KU₃.BABBAR 7 15 7 2.30 uš-taḫ-ḫat-ma kam DU₁₁.GA

(o ii 10) The king washes himself down over 14 sickles (made) of silver, 7 on the right, 7 on the left, and speaks thus:

o ii 1111

lu-u₂ ḫe-pu-u₂ ṭup-pi ar₂-ni-ia₅ lu-u₂ DU₈ru ḪUL-MEŠu₂-a

(o ii 11) 'May the tablets of my transgressions be broken. May my misdeeds be released.

o ii 1212

lu-u₂ par₂-sa lu-u₂ pur-ru-sa lu-u₂ pur-ru-ra gil₂-la-tu-u₂-a

(o ii 12) 'May my sins and my violent acts, all the evil in the mouths of numerous people, be cut off, may they be kept away, may they be scattered.

o ii 1313

u par₂-ka-tu-u₂-a NIG₂.ḪUL KA UN-MEŠ MEŠti₃

o ii 1414

ki-ma KU₃.BABBAR ina KA UN-MEŠ TI.LA lib-ši

(o ii 14) 'May (my) life (instead) be like (this) silver in the mouths of the people.'

o ii 1515

NUN ana ŠA₃ dugA.DA.GUR₅ KAŠ BALqi₂-ma kam DU₁₁.GA

(o ii 15) The king pours beer into a libation vessel and speaks thus:

o ii 1616

lu-u₂ bal-ṭa-ku-ma ina qa-be₂-e dUTU GIM dSIRAŠ ina KA UN-MEŠ TI.LA  ; : : : lu da-ar₂

(o ii 16) 'May I live and, at the commanding of Šamaš, may (my) life last forever like (this) beer in the mouths of the people.'

o ii 1717

NUN ana ŠA₃ dugA.DA.GUR₅ I₃.GIŠ BALqi₂-ma kam DU₁₁.GA

(o ii 17) The king pours sesame oil into the libation vessel and speaks thus:

o ii 1818

lu-u₂ ša₂-aḫ-ṭu lu šu-uḫ-ḫu-ṭu lem-ne₂-tu-a u par₂-ka-tu-u₂-a

(o ii 18) 'May my wrongdoings and violent acts be taken off, may they be stripped off.

o ii 1919

lu-u₂ el-le-ku-ma IGI dUTU TI.LA lu da-ar₂

(o ii 19) 'May I be pure and, before Šamaš, may (my) life last forever.'

o ii 2020

NUN 7 15 7 2.30 IGI dUTU ša₂ E₂ ṣi-bit-tu₂ u₂-šeš-šeb-ma kam DU₁₁.GA

(o ii 20) The king seats prisoners before Šamaš, 7 on the right, 7 on the left, and speaks thus:

o ii 2121

EN₂ ina an-ni-šu₂-nu a-qis-su-nu-ti aṣ-ur₅? ZI-MEŠ-šu₂-nu-ma

(o ii 21) Incantation: 'I have given them from their transgressions. I have protected their lives.

o ii 2222

u₂-kal-lim dUTUši dUTU u₄-mi-šam-ma u₂-ṣur ZI-i₁₄

(o ii 22) 'I have shown (them) the sun. Šamaš, daily protect my life.

o ii 2323

li-te-diš pa-le-e ina IGI dUTU ba-la₂-ṭi lu da-ar₂

(o ii 23) 'May he constantly renew my reign. May my life last forever in front of Šamaš.

o ii 2424

GIM KU₃.SI₂₂ i-dir-ti a-a ar₂-ši lu šar-ku-nim-ma

(o ii 24) 'Like gold may I not experience darkening. May long days of life be presented to me. May I receive (them) before Šamaš.

o ii 2525

u₄-um TI.LA GID₂.DA-MEŠ IGI dUTU ana-ku lul-qe₂

o ii 2626

mim-ma lem-nu ša₂ ina E₂.GAL-ia₅ GAL₂u₂ GAZu₂ lib₃-bi

(o ii 26) 'May all the evil that is in my palace (and) destroys my heart be put down at the dispensation of your speech and your commanding. May it be driven off. May it be taken away.

o ii 2727

ina ši-kin-⸢ti⸣ pi-i-ka u qa-be₂-e-ka

o ii 2828

lu-u₂ na-aš-kun lu ṭa-rid lu ta-bil

o ii 2929

lu-u₂ ṭup-pur lu-u₂ uk-kuš ina SU-ia₅

(o ii 29) May it be removed. May it be expelled from my body.'

o ii 3030

NUN uš-taḫ-ḫat-ma ana KUR KI.BAL TAG₄-MEŠ-ni

(o ii 30) The king washes himself down and they (the prisoners) go over to enemy country.

o ii 3131

EN₂ dUTU a-paṭ-ṭar UDU.NITA₂ ka-sa-a IGI-ka GIM ana-ku UDU.NITA₂ an-nam a-paṭ-ṭa-ru

(o ii 31) Incantation: 'Šamaš, I release a bound sheep before you. In the same way as I release this sheep, may transgression, guilt, sin and deficiency, the wrongdoings that are in my body, be released in front of your divinity.'

o ii 3232

ar₂-nu šer₃-ti gil₂-lat u ḫi-ṭi-tu₂ ḪUL-MEŠtu₂ ša₂ ina SU-⸢ia₅⸣

o ii 3333

ina IGI DINGIRu₂-ti-ka lip-paṭ-⸢ra⸣

o ii 3434

TA KEŠ₂ šal-ma EN₂ lugal šag₄ gin₆-bi kug-ga ⸢ŠID⸣-[ma]

(o ii 34) After the ritual arrangements are complete, you recite the incantation 'King, pure in true heart' and you greet the king and prostrate yourself. The king ... [...] a washing of the mouth ritual.

o ii 3535

LUGAL ta-kar-rab-ma tuš-ken LUGAL KA.LUḪ.U₃.DA x [x]

o ii 3636

ŠINIG ana ŠU-MIN LUGAL SUM-ma LUGAL ana IGI dUTU ⸢uš?⸣-[ken]

(o ii 36) You present a tamarisk to the hands of the king and the king prostrates himself in front of Šamaš.

o ii 3737

ina E₂ rim-ki NU NIG₂.SAG.IL₂e ša₂ ZI₃.DA ina [x x]

(o ii 37) In the bath-house you draw a figure of a substitute in flour on the [...], and the king stands on a ramp, and the mašmaššu-priest recites the incantation 'Utu from the great mountain' in purification, and he prostrates himself. The mašmaššu-priest ... [...].

o ii 3838

ḪURir-ma LUGAL ina UGU šuk-bu-su GUB-ma MAŠ.MAŠ ina te-⸢lil?⸣-[ti?]

o ii 3939

EN₂ dutu kur gal-ta ŠID-ma uš-ken MAŠ.MAŠ ŠU-⸢MIN⸣ x [...]

o ii 4040

EN₂ a₂-sag₃ a-gin₇ ŠID-ma A-MEŠ ana!(A) SAG.DU x [...]

(o ii 40) He recites the incantation 'The asag-demon like water' and [...] water on the head of ....

o ii 4141

A-MEŠ ana ŠU-MIN LUGAL SUM-ma EN₂ dUTU ⸢DI.KU₅⸣ [...]

(o ii 41) You present water to the hands of the king and you have the king say the incantation 'Šamaš, judge of [...] (and) the Underworld'. [...] ... behind him; you have the king say the [...] 'I give you [...] and my pardon'. [...].

o ii 4242

KI.TA-MEŠ LUGAL tu-šad-bab EGIR-šu₂ x [...]

o ii 4343

u pi-di-ia₅ SUM-ka LUGAL tu-šad-bab [...]

o ii 4444

LUGAL uš-taḫ-ḫat-ma x x [...]

(o ii 44) The king washes himself down and ... [...].

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

GIM an-nam ⸢ŠID⸣ [...]

(r i 1) When you have recited this, you wipe clean the king, (reciting) the ... '[...]'.

r i 22

LUGAL tu-kap-par ⸢EN₂⸣ [...]

(r i 2) (You wipe clean) a reed figure, (reciting) the incantation '[...]'.

r i 33

GI.SAG.DU.SA₂.A ⸢EN₂⸣ [...]

(r i 3) (You wipe clean) a scapegoat, (reciting) the incantation '[...]'.

r i 44

MAŠ₂.ḪUL.DUB₂.BAa EN₂ ⸢UDU⸣ [...]

(r i 4) (You wipe clean) [...], (reciting) the incantation '[...] sheep [...]'.

r i 55

EN₂ UDU.TI.LA UDU SIKIL.⸢LA⸣ [...]

(r i 5) (You wipe clean) [...], (reciting) the incantation 'Living sheep, pure sheep'.

r i 66

EGIR-šu₂ tak-per-ti eb-be₂-tu₂ ⸢LUGAL⸣ [...]

(r i 6) After that [...] the king with a shining purification ceremony.

r i 77

NIG₂.NA GI.IZI.LA₂ LUGAL tuš-ba-⸢aʾ⸣-[šu₂ ...]

(r i 7) You swing a censer (and) a torch past the king. [...].

r i 88

2 dugBUR.ZI.GAL SAḪAR₂-MEŠ A-MEŠ ⸢dug⸣[A.GUB₂.BA x x]

(r i 8) [...] 2 big porous pursītu vessels with water from a holy water vessel.

r i 99

gišEREN LI ana ŠA₃ A-MEŠ [...]

(r i 9) [...] cedar (and) juniper into the water.

r i 1010

2 mul-li-lu ina lib₃-bi GAR⸢an⸣-[ma]

(r i 10) You put 2 purifiers in the middle, and the king raises a purifier on his right and his left, and the king stands ... [...] of a long torch, and he pours [...] water 7 times to his right (and) 7 times to his left behind himself, and he speaks as follows.

r i 1111

LUGAL ina 15-šu₂ u 2.30-šu₂ mul!(NAB-)lil IL₂-ma x [...]

r i 1212

GI.IZI.LA₂ GID₂.DA LUGAL GUBaz-⸢ma A-MEŠ⸣ [x x]

r i 1313

7-šu₂ ana 15-šu₂ 7-šu₂ ana 2.30-šu₂ ana ⸢EGIR-šu₂ ŠUB⸣-ma kam ⸢DU₁₁⸣.GA

r i 1414

EN₂ DU₃uš de₂-a ip-šur ⸢de₂⸣-a 7-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma

(r i 14) He speaks the incantation 'Ea did (it), Ea undid (it)' 7 times, and you take 2 sky-birds, and you put a ring ... their [...] ....

r i 1515

2 MUŠEN ANe DAB-ma ⸢ḪAR?⸣ x [x x] x x-šu₂-nu GARan-šu₂-nu-ti

r i 1616

I₃.GIŠ BUR SIKIL? ⸢GA₂?⸣ [...] ⸢šu₂?⸣-nu-ti ŠUBdi

(r i 16) You pour pure bowl-oil [...] them.

r i 1717

ina 15 u 2.⸢30⸣ [...] ⸢MA?⸣ UZU U₃ [x] x ⸢ŠU?⸣

(r i 17) On the right and the left [...] ... meat ... [...] ....

r i 1818

ina UZU? x x x-a-tu₄ NINDA NU GU₇-šu₂-⸢nu⸣-ti

(r i 18) From the meat ... you do not let them eat food.

r i 1919

UM ⸢MA⸣ x x x ⸢EN₂ d⸣[UTU DI].KU₅ ANe u KIti₃ at-ta

(r i 19) [...]. The king [...] the incantation 'Šamaš, you are the judge of heaven and earth'. The king releases them (the birds) [...] sunrise and sunset, and after that the king speaks the incantation 'I have given them from their transgressions', and he releases the 7 and 7 prisoners who are held captive on the right of the king and the left of the king.

r i 2020

⸢LUGAL⸣ [...] dUTU.E₃ u dUTU.ŠU₂.A LUGAL BAR-šu₂-nu-ti-ma

r i 2121

[EGIR]-⸢šu₂ EN₂⸣ <ina> an-ni-šu₂-nu a-qis-su-nu-ti LUGAL DU₁₁.GA-ma

r i 2222

7 u 7 ša₂ E₂ ṣi-bit-tu₄ ša₂ ina 15 LUGAL u 2.30 LUGAL DAB-MEŠ

r i 2323

BARar₂ TA E₂ rim-ki ina E₃-šu₂ MAŠ.DA₃ ina GEŠBU SIG₃!(IGI)aṣ-ma

(r i 23) On his emergence from the bath-house he strikes a gazelle with a bow and recites the incantation 'On the lord's going to the steppe'.

r i 2424

EN₂ en-e an-eden-na du-a-ni ŠIDnu KU₆ BARar₂-ma

(r i 24) He releases a fish and recites the incantation 'Fish of the sea'.

r i 2525

EN₂ ku₆ a-ab-ba ŠIDnu KUR.GImušen BARar₂-ma EN₂ kur-gimušen kur-ta

(r i 25) He releases a goose and recites the incantation 'Goose from the mountain'.

r i 2626

ŠIDnu BIBAD⸢mušen BAR⸣ar₂-ma EN₂ bur₅ a-ab-ba in-tu-ud

(r i 26) He releases a duck and [...] the incantation 'The sea bore the locust'; you take [...] his left and walk in front of him, and the king speaks the incantation 'Ea, Šamaš and Asalluhi' : the apotropaic ritual for all evil.

r i 2727

[...] ⸢GUB₃⸣-šu₂ DAB-ma ana IGI-šu₂ DUak-ma EN₂ de₂-a dUTU  ; u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI : NAM.BUR₂.BI ḪUL DU₃.A.BI LUGAL DU₁₁.GA1

r i 2828

[...] ⸢na₄⸣AD.BAR GUB-ma u₂tar-muš ina KA-šu₂ u₂IGI.20

(r i 28) He stands [...] of basalt and, a lupin in his mouth, he raises imhur-ešrā-plant [...] his left (and) mihhu-beer on his right, and you set in place a huluppaqqu-brazier.

r i 2929

[x] ⸢GUB₃-šu₂⸣ mi-iḫ-ḫi ina ZA₃-šu₂ IL₂-ma dugNIG₂.TAB.TUR.RA GARan

r i 3030

[NU]-MEŠ ta-sa-dir-ma LUGAL kam DU₁₁.GA

(r i 30) You arrange effigies in a row and the king speaks as follows.

r i 3131

[x x] ⸢en⸣ en-en lugal lugal-lugal dutu EN₂ dUTU an-nu-tu NU-MEŠ  ; e-pi-ši-ia

(r i 31) The [...] 'Lord of lords, king of kings, Utu'. The incantation 'Šamaš, these are the effigies of my sorcerors'.

r i 3232

⸢EN₂⸣ dNUSKA šur-bu-u₂ i-lit-ti da-nu₃ EN₂ a-na-aš₂-ši di-pa-ri  ; NU-MEŠ-šu₂-nu

(r i 32) The incantation 'Formidable Nusku, offspring of Anu'. The incantation 'I raise the torches, their effigies'.

r i 3333

⸢EN₂⸣ ša₂ dUTUši man-nu AD-⸢šu₂⸣ EN₂ ša₂-ru-uḫ la-a-nu

(r i 33) The incantation 'Who is the father of the sun?'. The incantation 'The stature (is) splendid'.

r i 3434

⸢EN₂⸣ i-sa-a i-sa-a

(r i 34) The incantation 'Be distant, be distant!'.

r i 3535

⸢EN₂⸣ e-piš-u₂-a e-piša-tu-u₂-a

(r i 35) The incantation 'My sorcerors (and) my sorceresses'.

r i 3636

⸢EN₂⸣ ez-ze-tu-nu šam-ra-tu-nu

(r i 36) The incantation 'You are angry, you are furious'.

r i 3737

[x x] ⸢šer₂⸣-a-ni tu-kaṣ₃-ṣi-ri EN₂ KURu₂ lik-tum₃-ku-nu-ši

(r i 37) The [...] 'You have bound the veins'. The incantation 'Let the mountain cover you'.

r i 3838

[an]-⸢nam⸣ DU₁₁.GA-ma dugNIG₂.TAB.TUR.RA i-šet!(AL-)ma

(r i 38) He speaks (all) this, and he leaves the huluppaqqu-brazier to stand, and he takes [...] ... and walks in front of you and carries out a burning ritual, and the king speaks the [...] '[...] be released, great gods', the incantation 'All the curse', the [...] 'An evil [...] like a galla-demon', (and) the incantation 'Like this garlic'.

r i 3939

[x x] x MA DABbat-ma ana IGI-ka DU-ma šur-pa GARan-ma

r i 4040

[... paṭ]-ra DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ EN₂ ma-mit DU₃.A.BI

r i 4141

[...] ⸢ḫul⸣ gal₅-la₂-gin₇ EN₂ GIM SUMsar an-ni-i LUGAL DU₁₁.GA-ma

r i 4242

[...] MURU₂ ša₂ dUTU LUGAL GUB-ma EN₂ de₂-a dUTU u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI

(r i 42) The king stands [...] in the middle dedicated to Šamaš and he recites the incantation 'Ea, Šamaš and Asalluhi, what is my transgression?'. He stands in front of the ritual assemblage dedicated to the man's god and the man's goddess.

Right
r.e. 1r.e. 1

mi-nu-u₂ an-<ni>-ma ŠIDnu ana IGI KEŠ₂ ša₂ DINGIR LU₂ u diš₈-tar₂ LU₂ GUB

r.e. 22

EN₂ DINGIR-i₁₄ NU ZU u EN₂ DINGIR-i₁₄ be-li₂ ŠIDnu ana IGI KEŠ₂ ša₂ dbe-let—EDIN za?GUB-ma

(r.e. 2) He recites the incantation 'I do not know my god' and the incantation 'My god (is) my lord'. He stands in front of the ritual assemblage dedicated to Belet-ṣeri and [...].

Column i
r.e. i 43r.e. i 43

[...] ⸢EN₂⸣ bur₅ šag₄ abzu-ta EN₂ uš₂ ḫul-ŋal₂

(r i 43) The incantation 'Locust from inside the underground water'. The incantation 'Evil sorcery'.

r.e. i 4444

[...] ne₂-eḫ-lu EN₂ gu dšakkan₂-na EN₂ at-ta ma-[mit]

(r i 44) The [...] '[...] ...'. The incantation 'Cord of Šakkan'. The incantation 'You are the curse'.

r.e. i 4545

[x x at?]-⸢ta?⸣ u₂KI.KAL an-nam ŠID LUGAL UDU.NITA₂ ka-sa-a DU₈

(r i 45) The [...] 'You are the grass'. He recites (all) this. The king releases the bound sheep.

r.e. ii 1r.e. ii 1

[...] x KA LUGAL DU₁₁.GA-ma

(r.e. ii 1) The king speaks [...] ... and he recites [...] .... He sets (it) in place and he speaks [...] ....

[...] AN ŠIDnu GAR-ma

r.e. ii 33

[...] ⸢ID₂?⸣ DU₁₁.GA

r.e. ii 44

[...] DU₃uš

(r.e. ii 5) He adds [...] ..., dough (and) barley-flour, and [...] ..., he recites [...] ..., and he releases [...], and the king pours [...] ..., and the king speaks thus [...] ..., and he goes out [...] ..., and

[...] AN KA LI E NE ZA LI

[...] E NE ŠID-ma
